from PIL import Image, ImageDraw

# Colors
BLACK = (0, 0, 0)
DARK_GRAY = (30, 30, 30)
CHARCOAL = (50, 50, 50)
DARK_CHARCOAL = (35, 35, 35)
# Les Paul Cherry Sunburst
SUNBURST_CENTER = (220, 60, 40)
SUNBURST_MID = (180, 40, 30)
SUNBURST_EDGE = (120, 25, 20)
CREAM = (255, 248, 220)
CREAM_DARK = (230, 220, 190)
GOLD = (218, 165, 32)
GOLD_DARK = (180, 130, 20)
SILVER = (192, 192, 192)
DARK_SILVER = (140, 140, 140)
WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
RED_EYE = (255, 0, 0)
SKIN = (210, 160, 130)

def draw_les_paul_body(draw, cx, cy, scale=1.0):
    """Draw a proper Les Paul body shape"""
    # Les Paul dimensions (scaled)
    w = int(28 * scale)
    h = int(36 * scale)
    
    # Lower bout (larger, rounded bottom)
    draw.ellipse([cx - w//2, cy, cx + w//2, cy + h], fill=SUNBURST_MID, outline=SUNBURST_EDGE)
    
    # Upper bout (smaller, rounded top)
    draw.ellipse([cx - w//2 + 4, cy - h//3, cx + w//2 - 4, cy + h//3], fill=SUNBURST_MID, outline=SUNBURST_EDGE)
    
    # Fill the waist area
    draw.rectangle([cx - w//2 + 6, cy, cx + w//2 - 6, cy + h//2], fill=SUNBURST_MID)
    
    # Single cutaway (the horn) - Les Paul has cutaway on upper bout
    # This is the distinctive Les Paul cutaway
    draw.polygon([
        (cx + w//2 - 8, cy - 4),
        (cx + w//2 + 2, cy - 12),
        (cx + w//2 + 4, cy + 4),
        (cx + w//2 - 6, cy + 2)
    ], fill=SUNBURST_MID, outline=SUNBURST_EDGE)
    
    # Cream binding around body edge
    draw.arc([cx - w//2 + 2, cy + 2, cx + w//2 - 2, cy + h - 2], 0, 180, fill=CREAM, width=1)
    
    return cx, cy + h//2

def draw_les_paul_hardware(draw, cx, cy, scale=1.0):
    """Draw Les Paul hardware - pickups, knobs, bridge"""
    w = int(28 * scale)
    
    # Cream pickguard (teardrop shape)
    draw.polygon([
        (cx - 4, cy + 4),
        (cx + 8, cy + 4),
        (cx + 10, cy + 16),
        (cx - 2, cy + 16)
    ], fill=CREAM, outline=CREAM_DARK)
    
    # Bridge pickup (cream, rectangular with poles)
    pickup_w = int(10 * scale)
    pickup_h = int(5 * scale)
    px = cx + 2
    py = cy + 12
    draw.rectangle([px, py, px + pickup_w, py + pickup_h], fill=CREAM, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    # Pickup poles (6 screws)
    for i in range(6):
        pole_x = px + 1 + i * (pickup_w // 6)
        draw.ellipse([pole_x, py + 1, pole_x + 2, py + 3], fill=SILVER)
    
    # Neck pickup (closer to neck)
    px2 = cx
    py2 = cy + 4
    draw.rectangle([px2, py2, px2 + pickup_w, py2 + pickup_h], fill=CREAM, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    for i in range(6):
        pole_x = px2 + 1 + i * (pickup_w // 6)
        draw.ellipse([pole_x, py2 + 1, pole_x + 2, py2 + 3], fill=SILVER)
    
    # 4 Control knobs in diamond pattern (gold)
    knob_r = 2
    # Volume neck
    draw.ellipse([cx - 6, cy + 6, cx - 6 + knob_r*2, cy + 6 + knob_r*2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
    # Tone neck  
    draw.ellipse([cx - 6, cy + 12, cx - 6 + knob_r*2, cy + 12 + knob_r*2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
    # Volume bridge
    draw.ellipse([cx - 2, cy + 9, cx - 2 + knob_r*2, cy + 9 + knob_r*2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
    # Tone bridge
    draw.ellipse([cx - 2, cy + 15, cx - 2 + knob_r*2, cy + 15 + knob_r*2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
    
    # 3-way switch (cream tip)
    draw.rectangle([cx + 6, cy + 6, cx + 8, cy + 10], fill=CREAM, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    
    # Bridge (Tune-o-matic style)
    bridge_w = int(12 * scale)
    bx = cx + 4
    by = cy + 22
    draw.rectangle([bx, by, bx + bridge_w, by + 3], fill=DARK_SILVER, outline=BLACK)
    # Bridge saddles
    for i in range(6):
        saddle_x = bx + 1 + i * 2
        draw.line([(saddle_x, by), (saddle_x, by + 3)], fill=SILVER, width=1)
    
    # Stopbar tailpiece
    draw.rectangle([bx + 1, by + 5, bx + bridge_w - 1, by + 7], fill=DARK_SILVER, outline=BLACK)
    
    return cx, cy

def draw_les_paul_neck(draw, nx, ny, length=20, scale=1.0):
    """Draw Les Paul neck with fretboard"""
    nw = int(8 * scale)  # neck width
    
    # Mahogany neck (back)
    draw.rectangle([nx, ny, nx + nw, ny + length], fill=DARK_CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    
    # Rosewood fretboard (darker)
    draw.rectangle([nx + 1, ny, nx + nw - 1, ny + length], fill=BLACK, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    
    # Frets (metal wires)
    fret_positions = [3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18]
    for fp in fret_positions:
        fy = ny + fp
        draw.line([(nx + 1, fy), (nx + nw - 1, fy)], fill=DARK_SILVER, width=1)
    
    # Fret markers (trapezoid style - simplified as dots)
    markers = [5, 11, 17]
    for mp in markers:
        my = ny + mp
        draw.ellipse([nx + nw//2 - 1, my - 1, nx + nw//2 + 1, my + 1], fill=CREAM)
    
    # Cream binding on fretboard edges
    draw.line([(nx + 1, ny), (nx + 1, ny + length)], fill=CREAM, width=1)
    draw.line([(nx + nw - 1, ny), (nx + nw - 1, ny + length)], fill=CREAM, width=1)
    
    return nx, ny

def draw_les_paul_headstock(draw, hx, hy, scale=1.0):
    """Draw Les Paul angled headstock"""
    hw = int(10 * scale)
    hh = int(12 * scale)
    
    # Angled headstock shape (Les Paul style)
    draw.polygon([
        (hx, hy),
        (hx + hw, hy - 2),
        (hx + hw + 2, hy + hh),
        (hx + 2, hy + hh + 2)
    ], fill=BLACK, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    
    # Cream binding on headstock
    draw.line([(hx, hy), (hx + hw, hy - 2)], fill=CREAM, width=1)
    
    # 6 tuning pegs (3 per side for Les Paul)
    # Left side
    for i in range(3):
        peg_y = hy + 3 + i * 3
        draw.ellipse([hx - 2, peg_y, hx, peg_y + 2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
        draw.line([(hx, peg_y + 1), (hx + 2, peg_y + 1)], fill=DARK_SILVER, width=1)
    
    # Right side
    for i in range(3):
        peg_y = hy + 2 + i * 3
        peg_x = hx + hw
        draw.ellipse([peg_x, peg_y, peg_x + 2, peg_y + 2], fill=GOLD, outline=GOLD_DARK)
        draw.line([(peg_x, peg_y + 1), (peg_x - 2, peg_y + 1)], fill=DARK_SILVER, width=1)
    
    # Gibson logo area (simplified)
    draw.rectangle([hx + 3, hy + 2, hx + 7, hy + 4], fill=CREAM)
    
    return hx, hy

def draw_strings(draw, start_x, start_y, end_x, end_y):
    """Draw 6 strings""
    for i in range(6):
        offset = i * 1
        draw.line([(start_x + offset, start_y), (end_x + offset, end_y)], fill=SILVER, width=1)

def create_idle_sprite():
    """Player standing with guitar"""
    img = Image.new('RGBA', (64, 64), (0, 0, 0, 0))
    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
    
    # Character
    # Head
    draw.ellipse([20, 4, 32, 16], fill=BLACK, outline=DARK_GRAY)
    draw.ellipse([23, 8, 25, 10], fill=RED_EYE)
    draw.ellipse([27, 8, 29, 10], fill=RED_EYE)
    
    # Body (black metal outfit)
    draw.rectangle([18, 16, 34, 36], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    # Red chest accent
    draw.rectangle([24, 22, 28, 28], fill=SUNBURST_CENTER)
    
    # Legs
    draw.rectangle([20, 36, 25, 56], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    draw.rectangle([27, 36, 32, 56], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    
    # Left arm (holding neck)
    draw.rectangle([12, 18, 20, 30], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    draw.ellipse([10, 14, 16, 20], fill=SKIN)
    
    # Right arm (holding body)
    draw.rectangle([34, 20, 42, 34], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    draw.ellipse([38, 32, 44, 38], fill=SKIN)
    
    # === LES PAUL GUITAR ===
    # Body position
    body_cx = 38
    body_cy = 28
    
    # Draw body
    draw_les_paul_body(draw, body_cx, body_cy, scale=1.2)
    # Draw hardware
    draw_les_paul_hardware(draw, body_cx - 4, body_cy + 4, scale=1.2)
    
    # Neck position (angled slightly)
    neck_x = 22
    neck_y = 12
    draw_les_paul_neck(draw, neck_x, neck_y, length=22, scale=1.2)
    
    # Headstock
    head_x = 20
    head_y = 4
    draw_les_paul_headstock(draw, head_x, head_y, scale=1.2)
    
    # Strings from bridge to headstock
    draw_strings(draw, 22, 6, 42, 48)
    
    return img

def create_attack_sprite():
    """Player swinging guitar"""
    img = Image.new('RGBA', (64, 64), (0, 0, 0, 0))
    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
    
    # Character in dynamic pose
    # Head tilted back
    draw.ellipse([24, 6, 36, 18], fill=BLACK, outline=DARK_GRAY)

    draw.ellipse([31, 10, 33, 12], fill=RED_EYE)
    
    # Body angled

    draw.polygon([(30, 24), (34, 26), (32, 30), (28, 28)], fill=SUNBURST_CENTER)
    
    # Legs
    draw.rectangle([24, 34, 29, 54], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
    draw.rectangle([34, 36, 39, 52], fill=CHARCOAL, outline=BLACK)
